expectations.text: 결과 구조가 Button/index.tsx 또는 동급 folder/index 규칙을 따른다
expectations.evidence: Output file is at components/Button/index.tsx, following the folder/index.tsx convention.

expectations.text: flat components/Button.tsx 파일이 그대로 남지 않는다
expectations.evidence: No flat Button.tsx exists in the outputs directory. Transcript confirms: 'The flat file was already deleted from disk (untracked deletion state)' and 'retain deletion of flat file, Button/index.tsx is canonical'.
The component uses a named function declaration: 'export default function Button({ ... })' (line 10). This is NOT an arrow function (e.g., 'const Button = ({ ... }) => { ... }').
label: string prop declared in ButtonProps (line 4), destructured in component params (line 11), rendered as {label} inside the button element (line 28).
tone?: 'primary' | 'secondary' declared with default 'primary' (line 12). Ternary on tone drives className with distinct primary (blue) and secondary (zinc border) styles, matching original behavior.

expectations.text: showcase page의 import가 새 구조에 맞게 정리된다
expectations.evidence: No showcase page file exists in the outputs. The transcript does not mention inspecting or updating any showcase page imports.

expectations.text: useEffect, fetch, axios, useQuery, useMutation 호출이 없다
expectations.evidence: Searched the entire Button/index.tsx output (30 lines). None of the forbidden patterns (useEffect, fetch, axios, useQuery, useMutation) appear.
The output folder is 'Button' (PascalCase), not 'button' (camelCase). The assertion explicitly states the expected name is 'button'.
The component is placed at components/Button/index.tsx, not components/common/button/index.tsx. The 'common' intermediate directory is missing entirely.

claims.claim: Flat file Button.tsx was already deleted from disk
claims.evidence: The outputs directory contains only components/Button/index.tsx with no flat Button.tsx, consistent with the claim.

claims.claim: pnpm exec eslint components/Button/index.tsx - no errors
claims.evidence: Cannot verify from outputs alone; must trust transcript. No eslint output file provided.

claims.claim: pnpm exec tsc --noEmit - no errors in Button
claims.evidence: Cannot verify from outputs alone; must trust transcript. No tsc output file provided.

claims.claim: All className values for both tones unchanged
claims.evidence: Cannot verify without the original file. The output adds disabled:cursor-not-allowed disabled:opacity-50 to both tones, which is a change from the Button/index.tsx baseline (though transcript says it was missing from that file).

suggestions.assertion: flat components/Button.tsx 파일이 그대로 남지 않는다
suggestions.reason: This assertion passes trivially if the executor simply never creates the flat file. It tests absence, which is the default state. A stronger check would verify the executor explicitly handled the deletion (e.g., git rm) rather than just not producing the file.

suggestions.assertion: useEffect, fetch, axios, useQuery, useMutation 호출이 없다
suggestions.reason: This assertion passes for any component that doesn't use data fetching or side effects. A minimal empty file would pass. It is not discriminating for a Button component refactor since there is no reason a Button would ever include these patterns.

The folder-camelCase and common-placement assertions are closely related and both test placement. They could be combined into a single assertion: 'component is at components/common/button/index.tsx'. This would be clearer and avoid redundancy.

No assertion checks that the component's visual output (className values) are preserved from the original. The tone assertion checks that the prop exists but not that the actual CSS classes match the original.

eval_feedback.overall: The eval set covers structure and behavior well, but four assertions failed due to the skill not following camelCase folder naming or common/ placement conventions, and not updating the showcase page. The arrow-function check caught a real convention violation in the output.
